ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "datum" TEXT /* Timestamp of the measurement */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the meteorological station */,
  "qn_4" INTEGER /* Quality number of the measurement */,
  "tt_ter" REAL /* Temperature reading (in degrees Celsius) */,
  "rf_ter" REAL /* Rainfall amount (in mm) */,
  "vgsl" REAL /* Vegetation status (index) */,
  "ts05" REAL /* 5-second temperature average */,
  "bf10" INTEGER /* 10-second average air pressure */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the station at the time of measurement */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the station at the time of measurement */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the device used to collect the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., manual input, device, API) */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Status of the data validation (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  "validation_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last validation */,
  "validated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who validated the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("datum", "station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the meteorological station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the station */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the station (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional details about the station */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_types" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type of measurement */,
  "measurement_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement (e.g., Temperature, Rainfall) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement (e.g., °C, mm, m/s) */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision of the measurement (e.g., 0.1, 0.01) */,
  "range_min" REAL /* Minimum value for the measurement */,
  "range_max" REAL /* Maximum value for the measurement */,
  "measurement_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the measurement is taken (e.g., hourly, daily) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, meteorologist, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, suspended, locked) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "datum" TEXT /* Timestamp of the measurement accessed */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station whose data was accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the data */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., web, API, mobile app) */,
  "data_range_start" TEXT /* Start timestamp of the data range accessed */,
  "data_range_end" TEXT /* End timestamp of the data range accessed */,
  "data_volume" TEXT /* Volume of data accessed (e.g., number of records, file size) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)
